content:
Have you ever wondered how your body fights off germs when you get sick? Inside your body, there are tiny soldiers called the immune system that protect you. Vaccines are like special training games for these soldiers. 

When you get a vaccine, it shows your immune system what a germ looks like, but don’t worry—it doesn’t make you sick! This way, your immune system can practice fighting the germ safely. It learns how to spot and stop the germ if it ever tries to make you sick for real.

So, vaccines help your body get ready to protect you. They keep you strong and healthy, ready to play and have fun without worrying about catching certain diseases. It’s like giving your body’s soldiers a superpower to fight germs faster and better!
